title: Racosperma filipes
taxonomy: Racosperma filipes is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia. The classification of Racosperma filipes is as follows: it belongs to the genus Racosperma and is a part of the subfamily Mimosoideae within the Fabaceae family. The species is also known by its synonym Acacia filipes. However, its current classification may be subject to change as taxonomic research continues.
uses: Racosperma filipes, also known as the silver leaf, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
